summary:
The Waterlase system uses a YSGG (yttrium-scandium-gallium-garnet) laser based on its common use in dentistry, efficient absorption properties at 2780 nm, and industry standards for precision in oral surgery. To directly answer your query about which exact type was referenced here as part of this particular tool set within medical practice settings focused heavily upon minimally invasive techniques suitable across various tissue depths encountered during such treatments., it would be classified under D category indicating YSGG technology. ```plaintext
The Waterlase system employs a YSGG laser known for its efficacy in dental applications; characterized by optimal wavelength alignment and advanced features crucial for intricate surgical tasks requiring low invasion levels while ensuring thorough treatment outcomes.
``` Summary:

- The Waterlase system utilizes a YSGG laser primarily because of its suitability for dental procedures, offering precise control and minimized thermal damage.
- Industry standard practices support the choice of YSGG lasers for their effective performance in handling diverse tissue types commonly found in oral surgeries.
